instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `with-database-transaction` that calls `begin-transaction` on a connection, runs body, and commits via `commit-transaction` only if body returned normally; otherwise calls `rollback-transaction`. Use gensyms for the connection and the success flag.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 DB) (#:G2 NIL))
            (BEGIN-TRANSACTION #:G1)
            (UNWIND-PROTECT
                (MULTIPLE-VALUE-PROG1 (PROGN (INSERT DB ROW)) (SETF #:G2 T))
              (IF #:G2
                  (COMMIT-TRANSACTION #:G1)
                  (ROLLBACK-TRANSACTION #:G1))))
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 DB) (#:G113 NIL))
            (BEGIN-TRANSACTION #:G112)
            (UNWIND-PROTECT
                (MULTIPLE-VALUE-PROG1 (PROGN (INSERT DB ROW)) (SETF #:G113 T))
              (IF #:G113
                  (COMMIT-TRANSACTION #:G112)
                  (ROLLBACK-TRANSACT
